content.problem_latex: We have a triangle $\triangle ABC$ and a point $K$ on $BC$ such that $AK$ is an altitude of $\triangle ABC$. If $AC = 10,$ $BK = 7$, and $BC = 13,$ then what is the area of $\triangle ABC$?
content.problem_plain: We have a triangle ABC and a point K on BC such that AK is an altitude of ABC . If AC = 10, BK = 7 , and BC = 13, then what is the area of ABC ?
content.solution_plain: First, we sketch! [asy] pair A, B, C, K; A = (0, 8); B = (-7, 0); C = (6, 0); K = (0, 0); draw(A--B--C--cycle); draw(A--K); label(" A ", A, N); label(" B ", B, SW); label(" C ", C, SE); label(" K ", K, NE); label("10", C--A, NE); label("7", B--K, N); label("13", B--C, S); draw(rightanglemark(A,K,B,10)); [/asy] We now see that CK = BC - BK = 6. That means AKC is a 3:4:5 right triangle, so AK = 8. At this point, we can see that the area of ABC is 1 2 AK BC = 1 2 8 13 = 52 .

search.problem_text: We have a triangle ABC and a point K on BC such that AK is an altitude of ABC . If AC = 10, BK = 7 , and BC = 13, then what is the area of ABC ?
search.solution_text: First, we sketch! [asy] pair A, B, C, K; A = (0, 8); B = (-7, 0); C = (6, 0); K = (0, 0); draw(A--B--C--cycle); draw(A--K); label(" A ", A, N); label(" B ", B, SW); label(" C ", C, SE); label(" K ", K, NE); label("10", C--A, NE); label("7", B--K, N); label("13", B--C, S); draw(rightanglemark(A,K,B,10)); [/asy] We now see that CK = BC - BK = 6. That means AKC is a 3:4:5 right triangle, so AK = 8. At this point, we can see that the area of ABC is 1 2 AK BC = 1 2 8 13 = 52 .

content.problem_latex: One leg of a right triangle is 12 inches, and the measure of the angle opposite that leg is $30^\circ$. What is the number of inches in the hypotenuse of the triangle?
content.problem_plain: One leg of a right triangle is 12 inches, and the measure of the angle opposite that leg is 30^ . What is the number of inches in the hypotenuse of the triangle?
content.solution_latex: A right triangle with a $30^\circ$ angle is a 30-60-90 triangle.  In such a triangle, the hypotenuse has twice the length of the leg opposite the $30^\circ$ angle, so the hypotenuse of the triangle in the problem has length $2\cdot 12 = \boxed{24}$ inches.
content.solution_plain: A right triangle with a 30^ angle is a 30-60-90 triangle. In such a triangle, the hypotenuse has twice the length of the leg opposite the 30^ angle, so the hypotenuse of the triangle in the problem has length 2 12 = 24 inches.

search.problem_text: One leg of a right triangle is 12 inches, and the measure of the angle opposite that leg is 30^ . What is the number of inches in the hypotenuse of the triangle?
search.solution_text: A right triangle with a 30^ angle is a 30-60-90 triangle. In such a triangle, the hypotenuse has twice the length of the leg opposite the 30^ angle, so the hypotenuse of the triangle in the problem has length 2 12 = 24 inches.
